title: Phase 8.B: empty-registry UX — nag instead of silently fallback
description: Today an empty registry falls back to 'cwd-only single repo'. That hides the multi-repo feature behind discovery friction the user has to figure out. Change: when wyk starts with an empty registry, ALWAYS show a clear setup hint at the top of the TUI ('No repos registered. Run wyk init here, or wyk init -scan ~/Projects to discover all bd workspaces.') The cwd fallback still works but is no longer silent.

title: Phase 8.A: wyk init -scan <root> — bulk discover and register bd workspaces
description: Walks the filesystem under <root>, finds every .beads/ directory, and offers to register each into ~/.config/wyk/repos.json. Skips hidden dirs (.git/.cache/etc), excludes node_modules and vendor by default. Default behavior: prompt for each (Y/n); -y auto-registers everything found; -dry-run lists what would be added. Idempotent — skip already-registered paths. Solves the 'I just installed wyk, why doesn't it see my projects?' onboarding cliff.

title: Phase 8: multi-repo discovery + roborev-like density
Phase 5.B: wyk inbox — agent-side discovery of bounced-back work
description: The contract is symmetric: agent flags issue with 'human', human unflags it to send it back. But there's no agent-facing query/CLI for 'what got bounced back to me'. Add 'wyk inbox' subcommand returning issues that previously had the 'human' label but no longer do (or some equivalent unblocked-by-human signal). Output should be LLM-friendly: a structured listing the agent can ingest. Could use bd's interaction history or a new label like 'returned-by-human'. The recommended next move from the critique — closes the asymmetry between the human (TUI) and agent (CLI) sides of the contract.

title: Phase 5: post-critique improvements
Phase 4.B.2: multi-repo Source + table columns + per-repo writes
description: MultiBDSource holds a per-repo Client and queries each on Fetch, unioning results with Repo and Branch populated on each Issue (new json:'-' fields on beads.Issue, populated by the source not bd). TUI gains Repo and Branch columns at the front of the table when more than one repo is loaded; hidden when single-repo so wyk -C <dir> still looks compact. Writes (c/H/n/handoff) route through the Client whose Dir matches the targeted issue's Repo.

title: Phase 4.B.1: registry + enhanced wyk init (bd init + register + hook)
description: Add internal/registry: ~/.config/wyk/repos.json (JSON, version 1, list of {name, path}). Functions: Load, Save, Add (idempotent), Remove. Enhanced wyk init runs bd init if .beads missing, installs the post-commit hook (existing behavior), then registers the repo in repos.json. Idempotent end-to-end.

title: Phase 4.B: multi-repo support
Phase 4.A: table-style list with column headers + cleaner default preset
description: Two paired changes the user requested after seeing v0.1.0 running. (1) Rework the list view as a real table: column header row, fixed-width columns for ID, type, status text, priority, updated-relative-date; title fills remaining space; labels (HUMAN badge) on the right. Match the roborev TUI table style — clean alignment, header visible, status as colored text not just icon. (2) Change the default preset from 'all' (which includes closed items and clutters first paint) to 'ready' so users see actionable work first. Tab still cycles to 'all' for the full view.

title: Phase 2.A: write methods in internal/beads.Client
description: Add Close(ctx, id), AddLabel(ctx, id, label), RemoveLabel(ctx, id, label), and Note(ctx, id, text) to the bd CLI client. All writes must pass --dolt-auto-commit=on (per the saved bd memory: writes silently revert with the default 'off' policy). Refactor exec to make the runner swappable so tests can verify command construction without a real bd binary.

title: Rotate the staging database password
description:
The agent has detected the staging DB password has not been rotated in 96 days.

Steps:
1. Open 1Password vault 'Engineering / Staging' and locate the entry 'staging-postgres'.
2. Generate a new password (40 chars, alphanumeric + symbols, no spaces).
3. In Heroku, run: heroku config:set DATABASE_PASSWORD=<new> -a wyk-staging
4. Update the 1Password entry, then add a comment to this issue with the rotation date.
5. Close this issue when done.

title: Phase 1: read-only TUI MVP over bd JSON
description: Bubble Tea TUI: list view of bd issues, vim navigation (j/k/g/G), enter to expand, '/' fuzzy filter on title+body, dedicated keystroke that filters to label=human, preset filters (ready/human/mine/blocked/all) cycled by key, status bar with active filter and counts, graceful empty/error states when bd is missing or .beads is absent, refresh on keystroke and on a timer/file-watch on .beads. Read-only — no write actions this phase.

title: Phase 0: data contract — docs/CONTRACT.md and sample fixtures
description: Define the human-in-the-loop convention (human label + instructions in body + src:agent|src:human source label). Prove the convention round-trips through bd by creating three sample human-flagged issues and filtering to exactly them via 'bd query label=human'.

title: convention: nudge agents to ACT on inbox items, not just notice them
description:
## Why this needs you (please confirm this is accurate)

User feedback: the convention should explicitly tell agents that
non-blocked-on-human tasks (i.e. inbox items the human has bounced
back) are work to PICK UP, not work to note and move on from. The
current SKILL/CONTRACT text describes how to discover inbox items
('wyk inbox') and what their state means, but doesn't carry the
imperative — an agent reading 'here are items waiting for you' may
report them and continue with the user's current request rather
than acting.

I cannot self-verify this is the right phrasing without locking
in a specific framing — that's a convention call. What I tried:
re-reading skill 'Picking up bounced-back work' and CONTRACT's
inbox section, both descriptive not imperative. No workaround
without making the framing call.

## Steps

1. Add an explicit nudge to SKILL.md right after 'Picking up
   bounced-back work': if wyk inbox returns items, WORK them
   before continuing. Treat them as higher priority than tangential
   tooling work unless the user is mid-conversation about something
   urgent.
2. Add the same nudge to conventions.go (conventionsBody + a new
   field in conventionsStructured if useful).
3. Add to CONTRACT.md.
4. Update rememberedConventionMemory so bd prime surfaces the
   imperative on every session start.
5. Optionally: extend the meta-rule paragraph in CLAUDE.md to
   include 'and if inbox has items, work them before tangents'.

## What unblocks me when this returns

Agent-driven closure once the convention surfaces all carry the
nudge. No human artifact.

title: claude.md: treat wyk-experience feedback as wyk product feedback (file a bd issue, don't just patch the symptom)
description:
## Why this needs you (please confirm this is accurate)

This is a Conventions section meta-rule I want to bake into
CLAUDE.md so future Claude sessions opening this repo internalize
it. What I tried: this conversation surfaced the rule explicitly
from user feedback ('you should treat any feedback on the wyk
experience back to you as something that should be revised in
wyk'). The rule isn't anywhere durable yet.

## Steps

1. Add a one-paragraph entry in CLAUDE.md's Conventions section:
   feedback on the wyk experience (TUI rough edges, surprising
   defaults, missing affordances) is product feedback. The first
   move is to file a bd issue capturing the improvement; the
   second is to handle whatever immediate fix is needed. Don't
   stop at the symptom-fix.

## What unblocks me when this returns

Same as the sibling issue — agent-driven closure after CLAUDE.md
edit lands.

title: convention: document status lifecycle (deferred / blocked / open / closed) so agents pick the right one
description:
## Why this needs you (please confirm this is accurate)

I cannot self-verify that the current skill/conventions/contract
text is the right place for this — that's a judgement call about
what belongs in wyk's surfaced documentation vs bd's. What I tried:
re-reading conventions.go, SKILL.md, and CONTRACT.md, none of which
document when to mark a task DEFERRED. No workaround without making
a call about scope.

Concrete trigger: md3 (TUI screenshots) stayed open through the
v0.3.0-alpha cut even though the TUI is still WIP — open implied
'someone should do this now'. User feedback: should have been
deferred from the start. The convention doesn't teach this today.

## Steps

1. Extend conventions.go (conventionsBody + a Status field in
   conventionsStructured) to document the lifecycle:
   - open: actionable now
   - in_progress: someone has claimed it (--claim sets this)
   - blocked: waiting on another tracked issue; --blocked-by <id>
   - deferred: waiting on a still-evolving subsystem; revisit when
     conditions change. Use this for screenshots-on-a-WIP-UI,
     features-pending-design, etc. The UI will not surface these
     under 'ready'.
   - closed: done.
2. Add a Status guidance block to SKILL.md telling agents to defer
   over hold-open when blocked on subsystem stability.
3. Add a CONTRACT.md section formalising deferred.
4. Update rememberedConventionMemory so bd prime carries the
   lifecycle guidance to fresh sessions.
5. Tests pin the new schema field and the heading presence.

## What unblocks me when this returns

This is agent-driven — I'll close it directly when the convention
surfaces all document the lifecycle. No human artifact needed
unless reviewer disagrees with the structure.

title: skill+conventions: require agents to self-verify and label the unblocker before handoff
description:
Feedback: a handoff with a bare list of steps doesn't make the agent's reasoning visible. Two failure modes happen in practice:

1. Agent hands off something it could have done itself. The human reading the runbook has no way to push back beyond 'I think you could've done this' — there's no explicit claim by the agent that says 'I've verified this requires you'.

2. The runbook lists steps but doesn't say what the agent NEEDS BACK to continue. The human does the work, the issue gets bounced back, and the agent has to figure out from notes what it now has access to that it didn't before.

Fix: require two explicit sections in every handoff runbook, and make them callable-out to the human as 'review and confirm':

  Section A — 'Why this needs you (confirm this is accurate)'
    Two-line statement of what the agent tried, what blocked it,
    and the specific capability the agent lacks (auth, decision
    authority, physical access, third-party UI, etc.). Phrased as
    a CLAIM the human is asked to validate.

  Section B — 'What unblocks me when this returns'
    Concrete artifact(s) the agent expects to find on the issue
    after the human is done — a credential dropped in 1Password,
    a published URL, a decision recorded in the description, a
    config value committed. Without this, the next agent picks
    up the bounce-back without knowing what changed.

Places to update:
  - .claude/skills/handoff/SKILL.md: add a pre-handoff self-verify
    checklist; update runbook template + examples
  - cmd/wyk/conventions.go: extend conventionsBody and
    conventionsStructured to mention the two sections
  - cmd/wyk/init.go: update rememberedConventionMemory so the bd-
    remembered text carries the new structure into fresh installs
  - docs/CONTRACT.md: update the canonical contract text

title: tui: sticky header — viewport rows around cursor, never let terminal scroll the table header off-screen
description:
Symptom
=======
With more rows than the terminal can fit (registered repos with many issues, narrow window, etc.), the terminal scrolls and the column header row (W / Repo / Branch / ID / T / Status / P / Updated / Title) disappears off the top. The user is left guessing what each column means.

Cause
=====
The TUI renders every row in m.visible into View() and lets the terminal handle overflow. No internal viewport / scroll offset.

Fix
===
Introduce m.scroll on Model. On every render:
  - bodyHeight = m.height - chrome (title + setupHint + blank + header + footer/banners + status bar + breathing room)
  - render m.visible[m.scroll : m.scroll + bodyHeight]
On cursor moves (j, k, g, G), filter changes, and preset switches, adjust m.scroll so the cursor stays in the window:
  - if cursor < scroll → scroll = cursor
  - if cursor >= scroll + bodyHeight → scroll = cursor - bodyHeight + 1
Clamp m.scroll on every fetched data change (rows may shrink).

Add a subtle scroll indicator below the table when there are rows beyond the bottom ("+N more below" / "+N more above").

Where
=====
internal/tui/model.go — Model struct, viewList, the j/k/g/G key handlers, switchPreset, fetchedMsg.

title: cli: top-level help / usage should list subcommands instead of just top-level flags
description:
Agent feedback: 'wyk --help' currently shows only the top-level flag list (-C, -me, -probe). The subcommands (handoff, inbox, init, doctor, stats, registry, conventions) are invisible. Worse, the recommended path for filing a human task — 'wyk handoff' — is the discoverability gap that caused the feedback report.

Fix: install a custom flag.Usage that prints a structured help block:
  - one-line tagline
  - subcommand table (name + one-line purpose)
  - top-level flags
  - pointer to wyk conventions for the agent-facing rules

Where: cmd/wyk/main.go — set flag.Usage = ... before flag.Parse.

title: cli: new 'wyk conventions' subcommand printing the agent-ready tip
description:
Agent feedback: agents need a stable, scriptable way to learn the wyk handoff convention at session start. A subcommand that prints the agent-ready tip (label rules + bd create example + wyk handoff recommendation) lets an agent include 'wyk conventions' in their session-init dump.

Acceptance:
- 'wyk conventions' prints a short markdown-ish block: the two labels, the back-to-you handshake, prefer 'wyk handoff', and a concrete bd create example.
- '-json' flag for structured ingestion (a fixed schema with the labels + an example).
- Exit 0. No bd workspace required (this is purely about the convention, not state).

Where: new file cmd/wyk/conventions.go. Wire into main.go subcommand dispatch.

title: doctor: add a Conventions check stanza printing the canonical labels
description:
Agent feedback: the wyk handoff convention (human label + src:agent provenance) is only documented in inline source comments + docs/CONTRACT.md + the project-local skill file. wyk doctor is what an agent reaches for when something feels off, and it currently doesn't mention the convention at all.

Fix: add a Conventions section at the end of doctor's output (after the per-repo checks, before the OK/FAIL verdict) that prints the canonical labels and links to the docs. Always [PASS] — purely informational; not gated on anything detectable from outside. Keep it terse: 2-4 lines.

Where: cmd/wyk/doctor.go — runDoctor's output loop. Add a final stanza.

title: tui: drop issues whose ID prefix doesn't match the registered repo name (cross-workspace leak guard)
description:
Symptom
=======
domo-mcp (a registered workspace with a dead .beads/ — January-era jsonl-only export, no Dolt DB) renders in the TUI showing ebay-watchlist-watch's issues, attributed to domo-mcp. The IDs are 'ebay-watchlist-watch-iuv' etc., displayed as 'ebay-watchl…' because displayID can't strip the 'domo-mcp-' prefix from a foreign ID. Same four issues appear twice in the table — once correctly under ebay-watchlist-watch (with proper short IDs) and once under domo-mcp with the raw IDs visible.

Root cause
==========
bd-side: when a workspace can't load, bd's daemon appears to serve whatever workspace was last-warm in its state. Direct sequential 'bd -C /path/to/domo-mcp list --json' returns 'no beads database found', but under the TUI's concurrent multi-repo fetch, bd returns ebay-watchlist's rows for domo-mcp's query.

Even if bd fixes its end, wyk should not propagate this kind of mis-attribution silently. The right defensive layer is at the multi-repo decoration step.

Fix
===
In MultiBDSource.FetchWithSubErrors, after each sub.Fetch returns, validate that every issue.ID starts with sub.name + '-'. Drop any that doesn't, and add a FetchError for the sub explaining the foreign issues. The dropped count goes into the existing banner ('1 repo failed to load: domo-mcp') with a specific message.

False positive: a workspace where the user has manually set Name != bd issue-prefix. Document the failure mode and tell the user to edit ~/.config/wyk/repos.json. Same surface as the existing 'no entry named X' / 'unknown subcommand' UX.

Where
=====
internal/tui/source.go — MultiBDSource.FetchWithSubErrors. Inside the per-sub loop, after the existing 'if r.err != nil' branch.

Test
====
Stub source returning a mix of matching and foreign IDs; assert the matching ones land in 'all' (decorated with Repo) and the foreign ones appear in the FetchError.

title: init: wyk init -scan should probe bd before registering each candidate
description:
Alpha gate. Right now -scan registers any directory with a .beads/ subdir — even jsonl-only exports and stale shells (domo-mcp, will-you-kindly's empty .beads). The upstream fix for the registry-rot story.

Acceptance:
- Each candidate is probed with 'bd list --limit 1' (or 'bd query status!=closed --limit 1') under a 2s context timeout before registration.
- A candidate that errors or times out is SKIPPED with a stderr line: 'wyk init -scan: skipping /path (bd query failed: <reason>)'.
- Already-registered candidates still skip silently as today.
- Counters in the summary distinguish 'registered', 'already-registered', 'skipped (bd failed)'.
- Test that uses a stub bd runner to simulate one good + one bad workspace.

title: cli: wyk registry list/remove/prune for managing repos.json without hand-editing
description:
Alpha gate. Discovered during the registry cleanup pass that there's no first-class way to fix a misconfigured registry: stale entries (dead .git), nested registrations (android inside ebay-watchlist), broken workspaces (domo-mcp jsonl-only). Hand-editing ~/.config/wyk/repos.json works but is friction for early users.

Acceptance:
- 'wyk registry list' prints the current registry (name + path, one per line; -json for structured output)
- 'wyk registry remove <name>' removes the entry by name; exits 1 if name not in registry
- 'wyk registry prune' removes entries whose path doesn't exist OR has no readable .git anymore. Confirms with [y/N] unless -y given. Prints what it removed.
- Tests for each subcommand using a tempdir registry path.

title: cli: add wyk --version (and wyk version) so users can identify what they're running
description:
Alpha gate. Bug reports need a version string and there's currently no way for a user to get one without remembering what they go install'd.

Acceptance:
- 'wyk --version' prints a version string and exits 0
- 'wyk version' (subcommand) does the same
- Version derives from the Go build info (runtime/debug.ReadBuildInfo) so it picks up the install tag for module-installed builds and falls back to '(devel)' for go run. No hand-maintained const.
- A line in README under Install showing the expected output.

title: Phase 9.B: detail view shows bd notes
description: The detail view (enter on a row) currently shows only the description. bd issues accumulate context via notes (added with 'bd note <id> <text>' or 'n' in the TUI). Show them in a 'Notes' section below the description with timestamps. Requires extending beads.Issue with a Notes field if bd's JSON exposes them, or fetching them separately via 'bd show'.

title: Phase 9.A: 'N' key for quick-add a new issue from the TUI
description: A capital N keystroke opens a new-issue prompt (reusing the textinput infrastructure). Title is required; on enter, runs bd create with --type=task --priority=2 --labels=src:human (you filed it for yourself). Shows status banner with the new ID. Refetches so the new issue appears. In multi-repo mode the new issue is created in the cwd's repo, or the cursor's current repo if the cursor is on a row.

title: Phase 9: TUI completeness — quick-add + notes display
Phase 8.C: TUI density — match the roborev table style
description: The current TUI is functional but sparse compared to the reference (roborev queue): row density, column inventory, and persistent Repo/Branch visibility. Changes: (1) Always show Repo/Branch columns even in single-repo mode (the user explicitly asked for parity). (2) Add a Created column with absolute timestamp (matches roborev's Queued column). (3) Possibly: status counters in the header bar (open/in_progress/blocked/closed counts always visible, the way roborev shows Workers/Completed/Closed/Open). Output should feel like the same family of tools.

title: Phase 7.B: cut v0.2.0 release
description: Tag v0.2.0 covering the multi-repo support (Phase 4.B), Phase 5 polish (wyk inbox / handoff -create / hook chain / TUI badge differentiation), and Phase 6 (docs hardening, test coverage, wyk stats). Rename CHANGELOG [Unreleased] to [0.2.0] with date, add fresh [Unreleased] for any Phase 8+ work, push the annotated git tag, create GitHub release with notes lifted from CHANGELOG, update README install snippet.

title: Phase 7.A: wyk doctor — diagnose common setup issues
description: A 'wyk doctor' subcommand that checks the common friction points users hit: bd on PATH (and version), wyk on PATH (so hook works at commit time), registry exists and is parseable, each registered repo has .beads + .git + post-commit hook (plain or chained), .pre-wyk file present if chained variant is installed. Prints PASS / WARN / FAIL per check. Exit 0 if all PASS or only WARN; exit 1 if any FAIL.

title: Phase 7: wyk doctor + v0.2.0
Phase 6.B: integration tests for new write paths + invariants
description: Tests the Phase 5 commits skipped: (1) wyk handoff -create end-to-end happy path + the new orphan-warning path on simulated BounceToHuman failure. (2) Invariant test asserting both postCommitHook and chainedPostCommitHook contain hookMarker, so future hook variants don't silently break re-install detection.

title: Phase 6.A: docs hardening from design review
description: (1) Document the single-agent-per-workspace assumption of wyk inbox in CONTRACT.md (MED finding from job 1172). (2) Promote the orphan-policy rationale from cmd/wyk/main.go comment to CONTRACT.md (LOW finding). (3) Note the displayID truncation in the ? help overlay or README. (4) Mention the partial-failure-is-silent behavior of wyk inbox -json so LLM consumers know one broken repo doesn't surface as an error.

title: Phase 6: docs hardening + test coverage + wyk stats
Phase 5.E: README workflow narrative + screenshots
description: README documents install/run/keymap but not the day-in-the-life narrative. Add a 'Workflow' section showing: agent files issue + handoffs → human gets notified via 'h' jump in TUI → completes work → closes → agent picks up next issue. Plus markers for where screenshots should be inserted (user takes them; I can describe the captures).

title: Phase 5.D: hook composability — chain with existing post-commit hooks
description: Today wyk init refuses to install over a foreign hook. Real users (e.g. this repo with roborev's hook) can't use the auto-close. Better story: detect the existing hook is from a known third party (roborev, pre-commit, husky), chain wyk's logic AFTER it via an installable shim that calls both, OR fall back to creating .git/hooks/post-commit.d/ and writing wyk's hook there if the user has multi-hook tooling installed. Refuse only on truly unknown hooks.

title: Phase 5.C: file-and-handoff combined flow + skill update
description: The handoff skill assumes the issue already exists. Real agents more often hit 'I just decided this needs a human → file a NEW bd issue and immediately handoff'. Add a 'wyk handoff --create <title>' mode that does bd create + apply human label + set description in one step. Update the SKILL.md to cover this combined flow as the common case.

title: Phase 5.A.2: differentiate HUMAN badge by source label
description: src:agent (agent handed it back to me) and src:human (I filed this) currently render the same HUMAN badge. They're different user states. Render distinct: maybe a glyph differentiator ('→ HUMAN' for src:agent, '· HUMAN' for src:human) or two different colored badges.

title: Phase 5.A.1: trim repeated repo prefix from ID column display
description: Every visible ID in the table starts with the same 'would-you-kindly-' prefix (17 chars), leaving little room for the unique suffix in the 22-char ID column. In multi-repo mode the Repo column already disambiguates. Trim the displayed prefix to a leading ellipsis or just the suffix, e.g. '…-ma5.2.1' instead of 'would-you-kindly-ma5.2.1'. Single-repo view: same treatment since all rows share the prefix anyway.

title: Phase 5.A: TUI visual polish
Phase 4: post-v0.1.0 polish
Phase 3.C: cut v0.1.0 release
description: Rename CHANGELOG [Unreleased] → [0.1.0] with today's date (2026-05-28), add an empty [Unreleased], fix the CHANGELOG compare link to point at v0.1.0...HEAD, update README install snippet to pin to the tag, create the git tag v0.1.0, push it, and create a GitHub release. First proper tagged version of wyk — marks the end of Phase 0-3 build-out.

title: Phase 3.B.3: '?' help overlay
description: Add '?' to open a modal overlay listing every keybinding with a one-line description. The current status bar shows a compressed help line, but it's cramped and easy to miss. The overlay renders centered, blocks input until dismissed (esc or ?), and groups bindings (navigation, filters, writes, meta). Discoverability is the goal — the user should never need to read the source to find a feature.

title: Phase 3.B.2: ] / [ keys to jump between human-flagged issues
description: Add ']' and '[' to the keymap to navigate to the next / previous issue with the 'human' label in the current view. Useful when filtered to PresetAll or PresetReady and the user wants to triage just the handoff queue without losing context. Wraps at end / beginning. Status banner says 'no human issues in this view' when none exist.

title: Phase 3.B.1: true fuzzy matcher (sahilm/fuzzy)
description: Replace internal/tui/model.go's case-insensitive substring filter with a real fuzzy matcher from github.com/sahilm/fuzzy. The original brief specified 'fuzzy text filter'; substring shipped as MVP. The fuzzy matcher should rank by score so partial matches like 'rpw' against 'rotate password' work, and stable-sort results so the cursor doesn't jump as the user types.

title: Phase 3.B: TUI polish round
Phase 3.A: Claude Code skill file for handoff
description: Write .claude/skills/handoff/SKILL.md so a Claude Code session can discover and use the wyk handoff CLI on its own. The skill must tell the agent: (1) when handoff is the right move (judgment heuristics — auth steps, manual decisions, irreversible/political/legal actions), (2) how to construct a useful runbook (the description that becomes the handoff target), (3) how to invoke wyk handoff (the CLI shape, stdin convention, exit codes), (4) what NOT to use it for. The original brief implied this as the capstone but didn't include it in Phase 2.

title: Phase 2.C: agent skill — handoff package (BounceToHuman)
description: A separate small package (e.g. pkg/handoff) exposing BounceToHuman(ctx, id, instructions string) — which sets the 'human' label and replaces the description with the runbook the agent wants the human to follow. Intended to be the single call an agent makes when it wants to hand a task back. Keep the package small and dependency-free so it can be imported from external agent tooling.

title: Phase 2.B: TUI write actions (close / toggle human / note)
description: Split tui.Source into Source + Mutator. Extend keymap: c=close the cursor issue, H=toggle the 'human' label (handoff back / pull back), n=append a note (prompts for text via the textinput in a new mode). Add a confirmation step for destructive actions (close). Tests cover keymap routing and that mutator methods are dispatched correctly.

title: Approve the v0.3.0 release on GitHub
description:
The release branch passed CI and is ready for human sign-off.

Steps:
1. Open https://github.com/jimbottle/will-you-kindly/releases/tag/v0.3.0-rc1
2. Review the changelog entries and confirm no breaking changes are unmarked.
3. Click 'Publish release' to promote the RC to GA.
4. Close this issue once the release page goes live.

title: RETRO: tui: rename W column header to 'wyk' for self-explanatory naming
description:
## Why this needs you (please confirm this is accurate)

This is a retroactive bd issue for work I already landed without
filing one. The user requested 'update that name to be more clear.
even just wyk instead of W would be better than that'; I committed
fd1e514 directly. The meta-rule (cw6) was not yet in place at the
time, but the principle applied — every user-feedback request
should leave a paper trail in bd.

## Steps

(Already complete in commit fd1e514: header renamed W → wyk,
column widened to 3, check glyph centred under header.)

## What unblocks me when this returns

N/A — closed-from-the-start as part of the convention-audit pass.
Listed for the historical record so an audit of 'what got
implemented from user feedback' surfaces this row.

title: init: write the handoff convention into bd remember so bd prime surfaces it on every agent session
description:
Agent feedback proposed writing the convention into AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md at wyk init time so every session sees it. The cleaner channel for the same goal is bd remember: bd remember --key wyk-handoff stores a memory that 'bd prime' surfaces at every session start, no file edit needed.

Acceptance:
- wyk init (the per-repo path, not -scan) calls 'bd remember --key wyk-handoff-convention "..."' after the .beads/ init succeeds.
- Idempotent: bd remember with the same --key updates in place rather than duplicating.
- Skip when -skip-bd-init was passed (bd may not be available / desired).
- Skip silently on bd remember failure: this is best-effort enrichment, not a gating step. A WARN-style stderr line ok.

Where: cmd/wyk/init.go — after the bd init step, before/around the hook install.

title: docs: fill in CLAUDE.md placeholder sections (Build & Test, Architecture, Conventions)
description:
CLAUDE.md sections after the BEADS INTEGRATION block are still the scaffolding TODOs ('Add your build and test commands here', etc.). Future AI agents opening this repo deserve real content.

Acceptance:
- Build & Test: actual commands (go build ./cmd/wyk, go test ./..., go vet, the runner-injection pattern for swappable bd in tests).
- Architecture Overview: brief tour of cmd/wyk (entrypoints + subcommands), internal/beads (bd CLI wrapper), internal/tui (bubbletea model + Source/Mutator/Detailer interfaces), internal/registry (JSON workspace registry), pkg/handoff (the bounce-to-human convention).
- Conventions & Patterns: --dolt-auto-commit=on on every bd write, the Source/Mutator/Detailer interface pattern, the handoff contract (human label + src: source label + description as runbook), commit-message style (Conventional Commits with Closes: trailers for auto-close).
- Keep it terse — CLAUDE.md is consulted on every agent invocation; bloat there is expensive.

title: docs: real TUI screenshot(s) in README, replacing the TODO block
description:
## Why this needs you (please confirm this is accurate)

I cannot take a TUI screenshot. What I tried: looking for a
project-local screenshot tool (none configured), considering
whether stdout capture would work (no — it's a terminal-rendered
TUI with colors and box-drawing chars, not a flat string), and
whether spawning the wyk binary inside a headless terminal
emulator would round-trip into an image (none of the harness
tools I have can do this — they're file/code tools, not screen
capture). No workaround on my side: image capture from a TUI
needs a user-facing window manager and a screen-capture tool.

If you believe I could have produced screenshots — e.g. by
asciinema → svg, or by rendering View() output verbatim into a
PNG — bounce this back with H and tell me which path; I will
try it.

## Steps

1. From any directory, run `wyk`. You should land on the default
   `all` preset with rows from every registered repo, the `human`
   and `wyk` columns visible, and `← HUMAN` / `· HUMAN` badges on
   the human-flagged rows.

2. Capture a screenshot of the wyk pane. Include the title
   (`would-you-kindly` in pink), the header row, several issue
   rows, the status bar, and any banners. Crop to the pane.

3. (Optional) Press `enter` on a human-flagged row to open the
   detail view, wait a beat for notes to lazy-load, capture that
   too.

4. Save:
       mkdir -p docs/screenshots
       mv <wherever>/wyk-tui.png docs/screenshots/wyk-tui.png
       # optional second capture:
       # mv <wherever>/wyk-detail.png docs/screenshots/wyk-detail.png

5. Replace the TODO HTML comment in README.md's Screenshots
   section with markdown image refs:

       ![wyk multi-repo view](docs/screenshots/wyk-tui.png)
       ![wyk detail view](docs/screenshots/wyk-detail.png)   <!-- if you took it -->

6. Commit (the post-commit hook auto-closes md3 from the trailer):

       git add docs/screenshots/ README.md
       git commit -m "docs: add TUI screenshots to README

       Closes: would-you-kindly-md3
       "
       git push

## What unblocks me when this returns

Two files committed at docs/screenshots/wyk-tui.png (required) and
optionally docs/screenshots/wyk-detail.png, with README.md's
Screenshots section pointing at them. When md3 closes I will
draft the v0.3.0-alpha CHANGELOG entry and cut the tag — the
screenshots in the README are the last alpha-gate item.

title: ci: GitHub Actions workflow running go build + go test on PR and main
description:
Alpha gate. No PR signal that the project builds or passes tests. Minimum CI for alpha-quality.

Acceptance:
- .github/workflows/test.yml triggers on pull_request and push to main.
- Steps: actions/checkout, actions/setup-go (version pinned from go.mod), 'go build ./...', 'go test ./... -race', 'go vet ./...'.
- Runs on ubuntu-latest; matrix optional.
- README badge in the Status section linking to the workflow.

title: tui: surface per-repo Fetch errors instead of silently dropping them
description:
Issue
=====
`MultiBDSource.Fetch` runs each sub-source in parallel and `union`s their results. When one sub errors, the failure is swallowed: the bad repo just contributes no rows and the user has no visible signal that anything's wrong.

This burned us when a registry entry (`domo-mcp`) pointed at an old jsonl-only export that bd couldn't read — the TUI happily showed every *other* repo and the broken one was indistinguishable from a workspace with no open issues.

Fix
===
Two options, not mutually exclusive:

1. **Footer banner.** When any sub returns a non-nil error, render a one-line status (`1 repo failed to load: domo-mcp`) above the help bar. Same surface the create/close banners already use.

2. **W column states.** Extend the W column added in 2527757 to a 3-state glyph: ✓ (wyk hook),  (bd only), ⚠ (sub errored). User sees the failing rows are absent because of an error, not because they're empty.

Probably both — the footer for at-a-glance noticeability, the W column for which repo to investigate.

`wyk doctor` already finds these. The TUI just needs to stop hiding them.

Where
=====
`internal/tui/source.go` MultiBDSource.Fetch — currently logs nothing on per-sub error. Need a `FetchResult { issues, errors }` shape or a per-sub error slice the model can read.

title: doctor: handle .git as a file (gitlink) for submodule/worktree paths
description:
Issue
=====
`wyk doctor`'s post-commit hook check assumes `.git` is a directory and opens `<dir>/.git/hooks/post-commit` directly. For subdir registrations whose parent is the actual git root (e.g. an `android/` subdirectory inside an `ebay-watchlist-watch` repo, or a submodule), `.git` is a *file* containing `gitdir: ../path`. The current check fails with:

    open .../android/.git/hooks/post-commit: not a directory

…and doctor returns exit 1 even though the workspace is otherwise fine.

Fix
===
When `.git` is a regular file, parse the `gitdir:` line to resolve the real git dir (relative to the .git file's directory) and look for `hooks/post-commit` under that path. Two cases to cover:
- gitlink to a worktree/submodule (`gitdir: /abs/path` or `gitdir: ../relative/path`)
- worktree gitdir lives under `.git/worktrees/<name>/` — same parse, same resolution

Where
=====
`cmd/wyk/doctor.go` — the block currently doing `os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(dir, ".git", "hooks", "post-commit"))`. Extract a helper `resolveGitDir(repoDir string) (string, error)` that handles both the dir and file cases and reuse it in the hook + pre-wyk preservation checks.

title: Phase 6.C: wyk stats — handoff/hook activity metrics
description: The substantive deferred feature from Phase 5.F. Read bd's interactions.jsonl, aggregate: how many handoffs filed, how many bounced back, time-to-resolve for human-flagged issues, post-commit hook fires (rough proxy: count of issues auto-closed by the hook). Print human-readable by default with a -json mode for scripting. Crosses all registered repos like wyk inbox.

title: Phase 5.F: cleanups — wyk stats + PresetAll naming
description: (a) Add 'wyk stats' showing how often the post-commit hook fires, how often handoffs happen, time-to-resolve for human-flagged issues. Reads bd's interaction log. (b) PresetAll currently means 'non-closed' but the name is misleading — clearer would be 'PresetActive'. Either rename or add a PresetClosed/PresetArchived alongside for the 'show closed' case.
notes: Deferred to a future session. wyk stats is its own substantive feature — reading bd's interactions.jsonl, aggregating handoff timing / hook fires / round-trip latency, deciding on the output shape. Not a small cleanup; deserves a dedicated design pass. The PresetAll naming is documented and accepted in the README workflow narrative; renaming is churn for low gain.
close_reason: deferred — wyk stats deserves its own session; PresetAll rename is low-value churn

title: Phase 5.A.3: header separator + color calibration
description: (a) The underlined header row visually merges with the first data row when IDs/titles are long. Add a blank row gap or a horizontal divider char. (b) Status colors (ANSI 256 indices 39/214/203) render differently on Solarized/Gruvbox terminals. Either commit to truecolor hex values eye-checked against light/dark, or switch to named ANSI colors so user themes apply.
notes: Deferred from this session. Color recalibration is risky without iterating against an actual terminal screenshot — current ANSI-256 values render consistently across terminals (which was the implicit goal). If the user shares feedback that specific status colors are unreadable on their theme, this becomes actionable. Until then, leave 'em.
close_reason: deferred — color recalibration needs terminal-screenshot feedback to iterate against

title: Phase 2.D: wyk init + post-commit hook (deferred)
description: Lowest-priority piece of Phase 2 — bd already discovers .beads automatically, so 'wyk init' is mostly about installing a post-commit hook that auto-closes issues referenced in commit messages ('Closes: <id>'). Defer until 2.A, 2.B, 2.C land and we know whether the workflow actually wants this.

title: Decide retention policy for ephemeral wisp beads
description:
This is a decision the agent cannot make on its own — needs your input.

Background: bd supports 'wisps' (ephemeral issues subject to TTL compaction).
wyk currently surfaces them in the 'all' filter, which adds noise.

Questions:
1. Should wyk hide wisps by default in every preset filter?
2. Or only hide them in the 'human' filter (since a wisp is unlikely to be a handoff)?
3. Should there be a separate '--wisps' toggle in the status bar?

Reply by adding notes to this issue, then close it with the chosen direction.

title: Phase 2+: write actions, repo binding, agent skill (stub)
description: Out of scope for this session. Captures the future entry point: write/create/close actions in the TUI, an 'init' command for binding handoff to a repo, post-commit hooks, the agent skill that sets the 'human' label and writes instructions, and any daemon. Mark TODOs in code; do not implement yet.

title: Phase 5.G: deferred — repo column compression, hook bd-close coupling
description: Items deferred from the critique because they need usage data or are lower priority: (1) Compress the multi-repo Repo column when most rows are from one repo — section-header layout might be better; revisit after using wyk across 3+ projects. (2) wyk hook post-commit hardcodes the assumption that bd close is idempotent with --dolt-auto-commit=on; if a future bd version changes that, the hook breaks silently. Tighten the contract or add a probe at install time.
